_meta.description: 2026 events calendar including signature events, workshops, lectures, camps, and recurring programs

_aiRules.description: Rules for AI assistants editing this file
beforeEditing: ALWAYS read the full _meta section before making changes
ALWAYS check today's date and compare to event dates
ALWAYS verify the event is on Wed-Sat (open days)
ALWAYS use prices in CENTS (multiply dollars by 100)
NEVER fabricate event details - ask the user if unsure
NEVER delete events without explicit user permission
NEVER change fareHarborId without verifying with user
validation: After editing, mentally verify: Is the date on Wed-Sat?
After editing, mentally verify: Is the price in cents?
After editing, mentally verify: Is the ID unique?
After editing, mentally verify: Is the event in date order?
prohibited: Do NOT schedule events on Sunday, Monday, or Tuesday
Do NOT use dollar amounts for prices (use cents)
Do NOT add events outside the season (March 4 - Dec 20 for 2026)
Do NOT mark more than 10 events as featured: true
Do NOT remove the _meta, _templates, or _aiRules sections
_aiRules.whenUnsure: Ask the user for clarification rather than guessing

_autoDate.instruction: AI: Update this field whenever you edit this file

_meta.staffInstructions: HOW TO ADD OR EDIT EVENTS: 1) Scroll down to find the '_templates' section and copy the template that matches your event type. 2) Fill in your event details, replacing the placeholder text. 3) Add your new event to the 'events' array in DATE ORDER (earliest first). 4) Save and commit your changes. The website will update automatically after your commit is approved.

fieldGuide.id: Unique identifier using lowercase letters and dashes. Example: 'harvest-festival-2026' or 'spring-break-camp-1'. No spaces allowed.
fieldGuide.title: Event name as it appears on the website. Keep it concise but descriptive.
fieldGuide.date: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Example: '2026-05-15' for May 15, 2026. MUST be on a day Rocky Mount is open (Wed-Sat) for single-day events.
fieldGuide.endDate: End date for multi-day events in YYYY-MM-DD format. Set to null for single-day events.
fieldGuide.time: Event time as displayed to visitors. Examples: '10:00 AM', '2:00 PM', '10:00 AM - 4:00 PM'. Set to null if all-day or varies.
fieldGuide.description: Marketing description (1-3 sentences). Make it vivid and engaging. This appears on event cards and detail pages.
fieldGuide.speaker: For lectures only - the presenter's name
fieldGuide.speakerTitle: For lectures only - the presenter's affiliation/title
fieldGuide.fareHarborId: The numeric ID from your FareHarbor item. Find it in the FareHarbor dashboard URL when editing an item (Dashboard > Items > click item > look at URL), or in any booking URL. Example: if the booking URL is fareharbor.com/rockymountmuseum/items/236456/, the ID is 236456. When set, the website automatically generates the correct booking URL. Leave as null for free events or if using the general booking page.
fieldGuide.ticketUrl: Full URL for ticket purchase. Usually leave as null - the website auto-generates from fareHarborId. Only set a custom URL if tickets are sold on a different platform (Eventbrite, external partner, etc.).
fieldGuide.requiresTicket: true if visitors must book/pay in advance, false if free drop-in
fieldGuide.featured: true to highlight on homepage and featured sections, false for regular listing
fieldGuide.capacity: Optional - maximum attendees. Only add for limited-capacity events.
fieldGuide.pricing: Ticket prices in CENTS (multiply dollars by 100). null means not applicable for that ticket type. 0 means free for that category.

pricingGuide.howItWorks: All prices are in CENTS, not dollars. This prevents decimal errors.

categories.underFive: Under 5 years old - usually free (0) or null
categories.members: Rocky Mount members rate - often free (0)

pricingGuide.nullVsZero: null = that ticket type doesn't exist for this event. 0 = that ticket type exists but is free.

fareHarborGuide._purpose: How to connect events to FareHarbor online booking
fareHarborGuide.whatIsFareHarborId: The fareHarborId is the unique number FareHarbor assigns to each bookable item. When you add this ID to an event, the website automatically creates the correct booking link.

whereToFindTheId.method1_dashboard: Log into FareHarbor Dashboard > Items > Click on the item you want > Look at the URL in your browser. Example: dashboard.fareharbor.com/rockymountmuseum/items/562814/edit - the ID is 562814
whereToFindTheId.method2_booking_url: If you have a booking link, the ID is in the URL. Example: fareharbor.com/rockymountmuseum/items/562814/ - the ID is 562814
whereToFindTheId.method3_copy_button: In the FareHarbor Dashboard, some views show a 'Copy Link' button next to items. The ID is the number in that link.

whenToUseFareHarborId: Ticketed events that should have a 'Buy Tickets' button
Any event where visitors book/pay through FareHarbor
whenToUseTicketUrl: Event tickets sold on Eventbrite or another platform
Partner events where booking goes to their website
whenToUseNeither: Free events with no registration (set requiresTicket: false)
Community events where visitors just show up
Digital/milestone events with no physical attendance
fareHarborGuide.howItWorks: The website uses fareHarborId to automatically build the booking URL: https://fareharbor.com/rockymountmuseum/items/{fareHarborId}/. You don't need to type the full URL - just the number.
commonMistakes: Putting the full URL in fareHarborId - just use the number
Forgetting to set requiresTicket: true when adding fareHarborId
Using a fareHarborId from a different FareHarbor account
Setting fareHarborId for free events (leave it null instead)

rules: Single-day events MUST be on Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, or Saturday (Rocky Mount is closed Sun-Tue)
Multi-day events can span any days, but main activities should fall on open days
Keep events sorted by date - earliest first, then by start time if same day
Prices are ALWAYS in cents - multiply dollars by 100
Event IDs must be unique across all events
Use YYYY-MM-DD format for all dates (2026-05-15, not May 15 or 5/15/26)
Season runs from March 4 through December 20 for 2026
Featured events appear on homepage - limit to 8-10 total
Lectures should also appear in lectures.json with full speaker details
commonMistakes: Scheduling events on Sunday, Monday, or Tuesday (we're closed those days!)
Entering prices in dollars instead of cents ($12 should be 1200)
Using wrong date format (use 2026-05-15, not 05/15/2026 or May 15)
Forgetting to set requiresTicket: true for paid events
Adding events out of date order (scroll to find the right spot)
Leaving featured: true on too many events (dilutes the impact)
Copying a template but forgetting to update the ID

validation.howToCheck: Run 'npm run validate:data' in terminal to check for errors
manualChecklist: Is the single-day event on Wed-Sat?
Is the event in the right date order in the list?
validation.afterCommit: Website preview deploys automatically. Check the Events page to verify your changes look correct.

_templates._instructions: Copy one of these templates, fill in your details, and paste into the 'events' array in date order.
_templates._fareHarborNote: For ticketed events, you only need the fareHarborId (the number from FareHarbor). The website automatically builds the booking URL. Find the ID in FareHarbor Dashboard: Items > click your item > look at the number in the URL.

simpleEvent._description: Use for free events that don't require tickets
simpleEvent._fareHarborTip: Leave fareHarborId and ticketUrl as null for free events
simpleEvent.title: Your Event Name
simpleEvent.description: Write 1-3 engaging sentences about your event. What will visitors experience?

ticketedEvent._description: Use for standard paid events with general admission
ticketedEvent._fareHarborTip: Replace '000000' with your actual FareHarbor item ID. Delete the ticketUrl line - it's auto-generated from the ID.
ticketedEvent.title: Your Event Name
ticketedEvent.description: Write 1-3 engaging sentences about your event. What will visitors experience?

multiDayEvent._description: Use for events spanning multiple days (festivals, camps, etc.)
multiDayEvent._fareHarborTip: Replace '000000' with your actual FareHarbor item ID. The booking URL is auto-generated.
multiDayEvent.title: Your Event Name
multiDayEvent.description: Write 1-3 engaging sentences about your event. What will visitors experience?

lectureEvent._description: Use for lectures - remember to also add to lectures.json!
lectureEvent._fareHarborTip: If the lecture has its own FareHarbor item, add the ID. Otherwise, leave fareHarborId as null - visitors will go to the general booking page.
lectureEvent.title: Speaker Name: Lecture Title
lectureEvent.speaker: Dr. First Last
lectureEvent.description: Write 1-3 engaging sentences about the lecture topic.

campEvent._description: Use for kids camps - note pricing is child-only
campEvent._fareHarborTip: Each camp week should have its own FareHarbor item ID for proper tracking.
campEvent.title: Camp Name Week X
campEvent.description: Describe the camp experience. What activities? What ages? Lunch included?

limitedCapacityEvent._description: Use for workshops or special events with limited spots
limitedCapacityEvent._fareHarborTip: Make sure FareHarbor item has capacity limits set to match the 'capacity' field here.
limitedCapacityEvent.title: Your Event Name
limitedCapacityEvent.description: Describe the experience. Mention limited capacity to create urgency.

externalTicketEvent._description: Use when tickets are sold on another platform (Eventbrite, partner site, etc.)
externalTicketEvent._fareHarborTip: Set fareHarborId to null and provide the full external URL in ticketUrl.
externalTicketEvent.title: Your Event Name
externalTicketEvent.description: Write 1-3 engaging sentences about your event. What will visitors experience?

_examples._instructions: These are real events from our calendar showing different scenarios. Use them as reference.

freePublicEvent._explanation: A free community event - no tickets, open to all. Note requiresTicket: false and no pricing object.

example.title: Sullivan County 250
example.description: Sullivan County men left these fields for Kings Mountain. Their wives and children stayed behind, undefended. Hear the stories the textbooks left out.

standardTicketedEvent._explanation: A typical paid event with tiered pricing. Note prices are in CENTS (1200 = $12.00). Members get in free.

example.title: Harvest Fest
example.description: The smell of wood smoke and simmering apple butter. Experience the autumn harvest the way Tennesseans did it for 200 years.

childOnlyCamp._explanation: A camp with ONLY child pricing. Adult/senior are null because they can't attend. Note the high price (18000 = $180.00 for a week).

example.title: Summer Camp Week 1
example.description: Five days as a frontier kid. Fire a flintlock (blanks!). Cook over an open hearth. Ages 7-12. Lunch provided.

ageRestrictedEvent._explanation: An event with age restrictions. Note ageRecommendation field and underFive: null (they can't attend).

example.title: Haunting on the Mount
example.description: Candlelight flickers. Shadows move. Six evenings of spooky tours. Ages 13+.

_fareHarborExamples._instructions: These examples show the THREE ways to handle event ticketing. Choose the approach that matches your situation.

withFareHarborId._explanation: MOST COMMON: Event booked through FareHarbor. Just add the item ID number - the website builds the booking URL automatically. You found the ID '562814' in FareHarbor Dashboard for Harvest Fest.

withCustomTicketUrl._explanation: EXTERNAL BOOKING: Event tickets sold on another platform (Eventbrite, partner site, etc.). Set fareHarborId to null and provide the full external URL.
withCustomTicketUrl._whenToUse: Partner events, co-sponsored programs, or events using a different ticketing system

freeEvent._explanation: FREE EVENT: No tickets or registration needed. Both fareHarborId and ticketUrl are null. No booking button appears on the website.
freeEvent._whenToUse: Community days, free admission events, digital announcements

lectureWithGeneralBooking._explanation: GENERAL BOOKING PAGE: For events where you want visitors to go to the main booking page (not a specific item). Set fareHarborId to null but keep requiresTicket: true. The website uses the default booking URL.
lectureWithGeneralBooking._whenToUse: Lectures that share a general admission item, events added to FareHarbor later

events.title: Road to 250 Season Opening
events.description: The gates open on Tennessee's most historic year. Step through time to 1791, where government was born and a nation took shape. Be among the first 250 Tennesseans enrolled to have your name read aloud on July 4th—America's 250th birthday.

events.title: Spring Break Camp Week 1
events.description: Trade screens for sawdust and adventure. Campers dip candles, fire the forge, feed heritage animals, and learn skills frontier children mastered by age ten. Johnson City & Bristol schools week. Lunch provided.

events.title: Spring Break Camp Week 2
events.description: Trade screens for sawdust and adventure. Campers dip candles, fire the forge, feed heritage animals, and learn skills frontier children mastered by age ten. Sullivan County schools week. Lunch provided.

events.title: Sacred Scripture, Sacred War: The Bible and the American Revolution
events.speaker: Dr. James P. Byrd
events.description: America 250 Special Program. Dr. Byrd, author of the Christianity Today Award-winning book 'Sacred Scripture, Sacred War,' offers the first comprehensive analysis of how American revolutionaries defended their patriotic convictions through scripture. Explore the role of religion during the Revolutionary War. Co-sponsored by Bristol Historical Association, King University, and Rocky Mount.

events.title: Lisa Bennett as Mary Patton: Powder for Kings Mountain
events.speaker: Lisa Bennett
events.description: 500 pounds of gunpowder. One determined woman. Without Mary Patton's powder, there would be no victory at Kings Mountain. Through first-person interpretation, Lisa Bennett brings this overlooked heroine face-to-face with you.

events.title: Sullivan County 250
events.description: Sullivan County men left these fields for Kings Mountain. Their wives and children stayed behind, undefended. Hear the stories the textbooks left out—the gambles, the sacrifices, the families who risked everything.

events.title: Woolly Days & Colonial Gardening
events.description: Hear the shears clip through fleece. Watch raw wool become thread on a spinning wheel. Dig your hands into soil where heirloom seeds take root. Two days of fiber arts, heritage gardening, and the rhythms of spring on a working 1791 homestead.

events.title: Homeschool Day - Spring
events.description: History jumps off the page when you can touch it. Your students will dip quill pens, card wool, and hear stories from interpreters who've walked in William Cobb's shoes. TN Standards-aligned activities and take-home materials included.

events.title: All In: The Overmountain Men's Gamble
events.speaker: Thomas Batchelor
events.speakerTitle: Interpretive Ranger, David Crockett Birthplace State Park
events.description: They bet everything—their farms, their families, their lives. The 1780 march to Kings Mountain wasn't patriotic fervor. It was a desperate gamble by men who knew they might never come home. Thomas Batchelor strips away the myth.

events.title: Early Frontier Days & Colonial Trade Fair
events.description: Musket smoke drifts past canvas tents. Traders hawk pewter, powder, and handmade goods. Militia camps come alive with drill and fire. Three days of the largest Revolutionary-era gathering in the region, with OVTA. Bigger than ever for America 250.

events.title: Women Who Marched: The Untold Stories of the Overmountain Victory Trail
events.speaker: Jane Doan
events.description: While history remembers the Overmountain Men who marched to Kings Mountain, this lecture reveals the women who made that march possible. Part of Early Frontier Days.

events.title: Tennessee's 230th Birthday
events.description: 230 years ago today, the 16th star joined the flag. Tennessee's journey from territory to state began on this ground. LAST DAY to enroll in First 250—have your name read aloud at Rocky Mount on July 4th.

events.title: The Story of the American Flag
events.speaker: David Doan
events.description: Every star and stripe carries a story. From the Grand Union that flew at Boston to the banner that inspired Key's anthem, David Doan traces the flag's evolution. Followed by the dedication of Rocky Mount's America 250 commemorative flag.

events.title: Stitching Independence
events.description: Two days of stars, stripes, and stitches. Saturday: Tennessee's 230th recognition and our America 250 flag dedication. Sunday: the Betsy Ross Sip & Sew—wine, needles, and the art of flag-making. Limited seats for Sip & Sew.

events.title: Summer Camp Week 1
events.description: Five days as a frontier kid. Fire a flintlock (blanks!). Cook over an open hearth. Chase heritage chickens. Learn the skills that built Tennessee—then take them home. Ages 7-12. Lunch provided.

events.title: Father's Day Weekend at the Forge
events.description: The fire roars. The hammer falls. You and your kid, shoulder to shoulder, forging something real. Create a hand-wrought keepsake together at the anvil—a Father's Day gift money can't buy. 10 pairs only. Reserve now.

events.title: Summer Camp Week 2
events.description: Five days as a frontier kid. Fire a flintlock (blanks!). Cook over an open hearth. Chase heritage chickens. Learn the skills that built Tennessee—then take them home. Ages 7-12. Lunch provided.

events.title: Colonial Independence Day
events.description: America turns 250—and you're standing where Tennessee began. The First 250 ceremony reads each enrolled name aloud. Muskets fire. Period music plays. The flag rises. This is the day. This is the place. Don't watch history—be part of it.

events.title: Back to School at the Frontier
events.description: One last summer hurrah before the books come out. Kids race hoops, toss horseshoes, and play the games frontier children played. Adults sip lemonade in the shade. A family day with no screens, no schedules—just summer fun, 1791 style.

events.title: Homeschool Day - Late Summer
events.description: Launch the school year with history you can hold. Your students card wool, write with quill and ink, and meet William Cobb himself. Perfect kickoff to your homeschool year. TN Standards-aligned activities and take-home materials included.

events.title: First Families of Tennessee Reunion
events.description: Your family built Tennessee. Now come home. The first-ever gathering of descendants from founding families—Cobbs, Shelbys, Seviers. Friday: registered descendants only. Sat-Sun: open to all. Oral histories. Genealogy help. Stories passed down 250 years.

events.title: OVTA Victory Day
events.description: September 1780: Overmountain Men gathered at Sycamore Shoals and marched south to Kings Mountain. September 2026: we retrace their steps. Join OVTA as they commemorate the march that turned the Revolution. Date pending OVTA confirmation.

events.title: Harvest Fest
events.description: The smell of wood smoke and simmering apple butter. Cider pressing. Corn shelling. Sorghum boiling down to molasses. Experience the autumn harvest the way Tennesseans did it for 200 years—with your whole family, working alongside our interpreters.

events.title: Haunting on the Mount
events.description: Candlelight flickers. Shadows move. The frontier had stories they only told after dark—tales of restless spirits, unexplained sounds in the woods, and things that walked the Wilderness Road at night. Six evenings of spooky tours (Oct 15-17 & 22-24). Ages 13+.

events.title: Frontier Christmas
events.description: Cedar garlands. Dried apples. The smell of spiced cider. Before Hallmark, before Santa, there was simply the joy of gathering with family. Experience Christmas the way early Tennesseans celebrated—simple, meaningful, and warm. Wed-Sat through November.

events.title: Candlelight Christmas
events.description: A thousand candles light the grounds. Carolers sing in the cabin. Cider warms cold hands. Rocky Mount's most magical tradition returns—four evenings when the 18th century glows. Friday & Saturday, Dec 4-5 and 11-12. Sells out every year.

blacksmithWorkshops.title: Blacksmith Workshops
blacksmithWorkshops.description: Feel the heat of the forge. Hear the ring of hammer on iron. Shape red-hot metal with your own hands into a hook, nail, or blade—the same way smiths have done for centuries. You leave with something you made. All skill levels welcome.
highlights: Forge your own piece to take home
All materials and safety gear included

hearthCooking.title: Hearth Cooking
hearthCooking.description: No stove. No oven. Just fire, iron, and skill. Learn to bake bread in a Dutch oven, turn a roast on a spit, and simmer stew over coals—then eat what you've cooked. You'll leave knowing how frontier families fed themselves.
highlights: Recipe cards to recreate at home

twilightOnTheMount.title: Twilight on the Mount
twilightOnTheMount.description: Can't make a daytime tour? Join us Thursday evenings. A guided walk through the grounds as the summer heat breaks, with stories of the Cobb family and the government that began here. No crowds, cooler temps, and a different perspective on Tennessee's first capitol.
highlights: Guided walk through the historic grounds
Stories of Rocky Mount's role in Tennessee's founding

homeschoolDays.title: Homeschool Days
homeschoolDays.description: The textbook says 'frontier life was hard.' Here, your students churn butter, card wool, and write with quill pens. They'll remember this long after they've forgotten the chapter. TN Standards-aligned, co-op friendly, take-home materials included.
highlights: Educational materials to continue at home

behindTheScenes.title: Behind the Scenes
behindTheScenes.description: Hold a 230-year-old powder horn in your hands. Walk the collections vault where artifacts not on display await. Feed our heritage animals their morning grain. Coffee and pastries with curators who know every story. 10 guests only. Our most exclusive experience.
